title: Cherthala Karthyayani Temple
tagline: Cherthala Karthyayani Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Karthyayani Devi Temple, Cherthala is a famous Hindu temple located at Cherthala.
overview.description: Karthyayani Devi Temple, Cherthala is a famous Hindu temple located at Cherthala. Iratti and Thadi are the famous vazhipadu, Cherthala pooram is the second famous pooram in Kerala. Kalabham, in familywise, can be held on there, kalabham is a famous vazhipadu.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Cherthala
topPlaces.description: Cherthala is a suburb and satellite town of Kochi City located in the Alappuzha district of Kerala, India, along National Highway 66. As per the 2011 Indian census, Cherthala has a population of 45,821 people, and a population density of 2,727/km2 (7,060/sq mi).

topPlaces.name: Velorvattom Sri Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Velorvattom Sri Mahadeva Temple is a Hindu temple located in Cherthala in Alleppey district in the Indian state of Kerala. Believed to be constructed in the 7th century by Azhvanchery Thamprakkal.

topPlaces.name: Kuttikattu Sree Bhadra Kali Devi Temple
topPlaces.description: Kuttikattu Sree Bhadra Kali Devi Temple is one of the Bhadrakali temples in Cherthala, Alappuzha district, Kerala. It is about 1 kilometer west of the Arthungal bypass of NH 47 at Cherthala.

image.alt: Kuttikattu Sree Bhadra Kali Devi Temple

topPlaces.name: Kokkothamangalam
topPlaces.description: Kokkothamangalam is a census town in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Kokkamangalam
topPlaces.description: Kokkamangalam also known as Gokkamangalam is a village in Alappuzha district of Kerala state, south India. It is situated in between Cochin and Kumarakom and on the western shore of Vembanad Lake, 5 km east of the town of Cherthala, which has the nearest railway station.

topPlaces.name: Cherthala North
topPlaces.description: Cherthala North is a village in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: St. Thomas Church, Kokkamangalam
topPlaces.description: St. Thomas Syro-Malabar Catholic Church, Kokkamangalam, popularly known as Kokkamangalam Church, which holds a midway position among the seven churches founded by St.

topPlaces.name: Vayalar
topPlaces.description: Vayalar (Malayalam: [ʋɐjɐlaːr]) is a village in Cherthala taluk, Alapuzha district, Kerala state, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Cherthala Karthyayani Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Cherthala Karthyayani Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Cherthala Karthyayani Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (52 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (32 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Cherthala Karthyayani Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Cherthala Karthyayani Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Cherthala Karthyayani Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Cherthala Karthyayani Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Cherthala Karthyayani Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Cherthala, Velorvattom Sri Mahadeva Temple, Kuttikattu Sree Bhadra Kali Devi Temple, Kokkothamangalam, Kokkamangalam.
